node.statement: N5 (FORWARD: P is an exact signed affine retraction). Define P:=theta(2Q-I)=(1/2)(I+sgn(S)) where S=2Q-I (N2), sgn(S)=S R (N4). This is exactly the GT-kitaev-spectral-propP construction with the renaming P_Kitaev->Q, delta->eta (||Q^2-Q||<=eta<1/4, GT-hyp): Prop_P gives ~P=theta(2Q-I) with ~P^2=~P EXACTLY. We re-derive idempotence directly to keep it self-contained: P^2=(1/4)(I+sgn(S))^2=(1/4)(I+2 sgn(S)+sgn(S)^2)=(1/4)(I+2 sgn(S)+I)=(1/4)(2I+2 sgn(S))=(1/2)(I+sgn(S))=P, using sgn(S)^2=I (N4). Unit-preservation: P1=(1/2)(1+sgn(S)1)=(1/2)(1+S(R1))=(1/2)(1+S*1)=(1/2)(1+S1)=(1/2)(1+1)=1, using R1=1 (N3) and S1=1 (N2). So P1=1 and P^2=P exactly; P's rows are signed measures (real entries) of total mass 1 (from P1=1), i.e. P is a signed affine retraction in the sense of def-stochastic. [GT-kitaev-spectral-propP; N2; N3; N4]
